text: Welcome to SRM Hostels Book Hostel Contact Us Home SRM Hostels All about Student accommodation & facilities We, at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University), provide a wide choice in selecting the type or rooms in a hostel to suit every budget, with an aim of providing a decent stay and the best possible learning environment. The quality of life is one of the most important factors considered by the students deciding to stay in the College/University hostels. By quality, it is not just the rooms but also the overall environment of the University campus. Ours is one of the greenest Campuses’ in the country with all the facilities that are expected from global Universities. The stay at our campus provides a wealth of experience by developing qualities such as camaraderie and social responsibility. Our students comprise a cosmopolitan mix from all parts of India as well as many countries across the globe. The students staying at SRM hostels are provided with healthy and sumptuous food options at highly subsidized rates. They can choose to have North Indian or South Indian menu in the dining halls that are co-located. For the benefit of our international students, the hostels also offer a multi-cuisine dining facility providing Thai, Chinese and continental food. Life on campus helps the students not only to study but also to socialize with the peers. Friendships formed on the campus last a lifetime. SRM provides a comfortable and safe housing to all its students. The students housed in the campus are encouraged to use the gymnasium, playground, and all indoor gaming facilities. Dr. E. Poovammal Directorate of Hostels SRMIST-KTR Campus director.hostels@srmist.edu.in +91 9940036021 Director's Message As per our Founder Chancellor’s wish, the mission of our directorate is to provide a safe, supportive, and comfortable living environment that fosters academic success and personal growth of every SRMISTian. We encourage you to take care of the hostel facilities and report any concerns or needs for improvements. Should you have any personal, academic, or social concerns, our hostel staff, including Wardens and support personnel, are always available to assist. We also encourage students to participate in the various cultural, recreational, and educational activities hosted by the Directorate throughout the year. “ A hostel is not a place: It is a state of mind- open, adventurous and full of possibilities ” Meet Our Staff Hostel Rules Vision

Centre for Statistics Home Research Research Wings Centre for Statistics Overview Centre for Statistics is a hub of statistical training, research, and consultancy at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur. Our aim is to unite multidisciplinary students, research scholars, and faculty to foster innovative thinking and collaboration, as well as address new research challenges across various disciplines within our institution. As a leader in statistical applications and data science, the Centre represents a significant step forward for our institution. Vision Pursue Excellence in Statistical Training and Research Serve as an Epicenter for Statistical Activities within the Institute Develop a Dynamic Partnership Between Academia and Industry Promote the Use of Statistics Across Various Fields Mission To make numbers speak and transform data into actionable insights, for advancing statistical knowledge and contributing to the development of academic research and industrial collaborations. 2000 Established 100 Consultancy Services 1 Training Programs 1 Publications 1 Funded Projects Professor and Head Email: head.cfs@srmist.edu.in Welcome to Centre for Statistics The field of statistics is increasingly essential in today’s world due to its vital role in fostering evidence-based decision-making. This discipline supports decision-makers, policymakers, researchers, government and private agencies in reshaping society through informed choices. With the advent of modern computing and technological advancements, the importance and application of statistics have expanded significantly, benefiting students and practitioners alike. Centre for Statistics in SRM Institute of Science and Technology, Kattankulathur demonstrates the intent to benefit the student and scholar community, and faculty members with world-class assistance in this subject. In addition to helping students acquire data analytic skills, the centre provides in-depth knowledge regarding the application of statistical techniques through effective supervisory training programs. Also to push forward the frontiers of knowledge by offering statistical consultancies and interacting with industries. We encourage students, research scholars, and faculty members to utilise the resources and expertise available at the Centre for Statistics. You can find us in Room No.1303, 13th Floor, University Building. For any inquiries or further information, please write to us at statcentre.ktr@srmist.edu.in . We provide tailored sessions for internal and external participants, including faculty, researchers, and students. Our focus is on delivering high-quality, practical knowledge to all levels of proficiency. Data Analytic Unit Our Data Analytic Unit specializes in translating complex research questions into clear statistical problems. We work closely with researchers to provide precise and actionable insights through advanced statistical analysis and methodological expertise. Our goal is to support a wide range of research needs through interdisciplinary collaboration and expert consultation. Statistical Consultancy Unit The Statistical Consultancy Unit offers professional statistical services on a fee-for-service basis. We assist with grant writing, questionnaire design, survey methodology, data analysis, result interpretation, and report writing. Our services are aimed at providing precise and impactful solutions to support your research and data needs. text: Getting around & Safety Home Life at SRM Getting Around & Campus Safety Getting around & Safety Our Leadership’s vision is to ensure top quality student life in a well maintained serene safe and secure campus. We constantly keep maintaining and improving the upkeep of our campus. It is the Leadership vision, support and our efforts that make SRMIST the best campus with lakes, greenery, bio-diversity and is a source of many wonderful memories and experiences for most of our students, staff, residents and visitors. Policies - SRM Green Initiatives Policy on Transportation Facility Use of Bicycles Barrier Free Environment Ban on Plastic Landscaping Pedestrian Walkway Policy Restricted Entry of Automobile Serene Green campus This sprawling 250+ acres has in its midst an old world charm on the one end (main campus) to the ultra modern buildings, gardens, lawns, sitout areas, cafes and walkways with trees on the either side giving a wonderful ambience to students and staff. The lakes around, gardens and greenery all around give a perfect balance to the life at SRMIST. Do visit our Campus here at Kattankulathur to get a first hand feel of our green campus! Cycling across the campus This is a delight to cyclists who not only gain good health but also find it absolutely relaxing to ride along the length and breadth of this campus. There are a variety of flora and fauna as well as roads named after inspiring personalities from across India and all over the world. While one is at it, don’t miss out on the blending of ancient with the modern as you see monolithic statutes of Raja Raja Chola, Swami Vivekananda, Mahatma Gandhi, Thiruvalluvar, Socrates, Anna, among others. The upcoming Vendhar Square will be a big attraction in the coming months! Transport Facility Such a vast campus does require our students and staff to move from one location to another and to enable easier transit, we have our SRM Coaches to the service. Not just within the campus, the transport facility is optional for students and faculty to come to the campus from Chennai and near by areas and get dropped back to their localities. We operate 100s of buses, AC and Non-AC buses, to ensure timely and convenient transit to and back from the campus. Easy and Convenient Accessibility for All The goal for SRMIST is to constantly review and invest in removing barriers to create an inclusive and accessible campus for all. Lack of easy access is the greatest road block that prevents empowerment of people with Disabilities (PwD). Barrier free environment enables the PwD to develop in the field of their interest. Towards this, SRMIST provides ramps, low floors in buses, elevators to reach classrooms and labs, Battery run cars, wheelchair friendly network, among others. Earthquake Research Cell (EQRC) Home Research Research Wings EQRC Overview SRMIST established an Earthquake Research cell (EQRC) in July 2012 for Advanced Research on the causes for origin, occurrence and prediction of Earthquake by better understanding the geodynamical behavior of this natural disaster on the globe. Further to establish the non-plate tectonics causes for the earthquake through re-looking into the existing causes and to develop computer simulation model for geodynamic character, prediction, prevention and management model to reduce the damages on the Earth. Vision To be a Global Centre of Excellence in Earthquake Research, Disaster Management and Mitigation studies Mission To be a leading Centre for Earthquake Research of international repute for the investigation of Geodynamic activity. To enhance the knowledge based on research, education and information transfer to reduce the damages through systematic understanding of seismological characters. To provide capacity building to mitigate future earthquake scenario for better planning and development. Dr. R. Sivakumar Co-ordinator Phone: 044 - 27455510, 47437500 Email: eqr.cell.ktr@srmist.edu.in Welcome to EQRC SRMIST is among the first few institutions in the country to establish the Earthquake Research Centre, thanks to the foresight of our Founder & Chancellor Dr. T R Paarivendhar. Our Centre’s focus in terms of geographic regions are Sikkim – Darjeeling, Himalaya region in the North East, Andaman Region (islands in the Bay of Bengal), Ponnair Basin area in the South and Gujarat in the West. We have 12 themes that we are actively pursuing at EQRC, SRMIST. The Researchers are senior and tenured specialists in various practice areas who contribute to the furthering of our research activity. Our students and scholars who get to work at our EQRC stand to benefit immensely from our senior Faculty Members’ guidance and applied (on-the-field) learning. At EQRC, it is our endeavor to accomplish breakthrough research and contribute new ideas, concepts and solutions. Dr. S. S. Sridhar Professor, Department of Computing Technologies, SRMIST Research Theme Our research themes allow us to build on our existing strengths and invest in novel complementary areas. EQRC’s advantage is that our research is not confined to our center and there is a sizable proportion of our centre’s activity involving work on-field. Major themes of our research are as follows: Seismic hazard assessment Active tectonic study - Identification of Active fault zones. Geomorphic and morphotectonic anomaly- analysis of geomorphic indices, paleo-seismic evidences Crustal deformation studies and development of deformation model and 3D

contribute new ideas, concepts and solutions. Dr. S. S. Sridhar Professor, Department of Computing Technologies, SRMIST Research Theme Our research themes allow us to build on our existing strengths and invest in novel complementary areas. EQRC’s advantage is that our research is not confined to our center and there is a sizable proportion of our centre’s activity involving work on-field. Major themes of our research are as follows: Seismic hazard assessment Active tectonic study - Identification of Active fault zones. Geomorphic and morphotectonic anomaly- analysis of geomorphic indices, paleo-seismic evidences Crustal deformation studies and development of deformation model and 3D visualization. Analysis of epicenter data and prediction through ANN model. Quantification of seismic energy and simulation model Mathematical model through continuum / regression model. Remote sensing based surface fault mapping and geotectonic analysis. GIS based seismic database creation. Development of customized Seismic Information System. Designing seismic resistant structures. Disaster planning and Management Focused Areas Sikkim- Darjeeling Himalaya, N-E region Andaman Region Ponnair basin, South India Gujarat, western India Research Highlights Facilities Our world-class facilities make SRMIST one of the India’s best universities for research and study. SRMIST is lead by the visionary Founder and Chancellor who has embedded research orientation and have supported us through investing in a wide range of facilities and resources to support our cutting-edge research. Whether it is subscription to internationally important knowledge inputs and reference material to state of the art labs, we have it all at our EQRC. Corporate Organizations and academics are invited to make use of our facilities and research services, including using our labs and equipment and consultancy services. Following is a selection of our research facilities Facilities Publications & Patents The experiential insights and research outcomes are often carried for publication in the nationally and internationally renouned Journals. Our researchers get to submit and present papers among the specialists from across the world on specialized topics connected to Earthquake Research. SRMIST is a platform for anybody seeking to create a dent through their commitment and hard work as we can count on some of the most valuable and best research ecosystem. This automatically means there are so many opportunities to pick, prepare and submit papers. We are proud that our faculty members and alumni have published book-chapters and books that are often reference literature to the current day students and research scholars worldwide. High Performance Computing Center (HPCC) Home Research HPCC Overview A High-Performance Computing Center is a location with a High-performance Computers (HPC) facility usually needed for research work. A high-performance computer (HPC) is a computer with a high-level computational capacity compared to a general-purpose computer. The performance of High-performance computers (HPC) is measured in floating-point operations per second (FLOPS) instead of million instructions per second (MIPS). In general thousands of dedicated processors are placed in proximity to each other (e.g. in a computer cluster); this saves considerable time moving data around and makes it possible for the processors to work together (rather than on separate tasks), for example in mesh and hypercube architectures. High-performance computers (HPC) are typically used for scientific and engineering applications that must handle very large databases or do a great amount of computation (or both). Core Research Areas Computational Materials Science Computational Chemistry Big-Data Analysis and Data Science Genetic Engineering Atmospheric Science Computational Mechanics and Fluid Dynamics Who can use Any faculty, members and students of SRMIST can potentially use this High-performance Computing facility, based on their needs. Vision To establish world-class High-performance Computing facility to promote the computational research in different scientific and technological area to solve the current and future challenges for betterment of humanity Mission Collaborative project and alliance with different world class research groups. Training Program, creating e-learning database and series of hands-on session to develop computational skill among the students and faculties of the university. Targeted research areas to strengthen the centre Dr. D. Prof. Mohana Krishnan R Receives “Excellence in Sports Leadership & Governance Award” at Award for Excellence in Sports 2025 Home Prof. Mohana Krishnan R Receives “Excellence in Sports Leadership & Governance Award” at Award for Excellence in Sports 2025 Prof. Mohana Krishnan R, Director, Directorate of Sports SRMIST, receiving the Certificate of Honour for Excellence in Sports Leadership & Governance Award from the Physical Education Foundation of India (PEFI) at the Award for Excellence in Sports 2025 at Tiruchirappalli, presented by Shri Kamal Haasan, Member of Parliament, Rajya Sabha, and Thiru Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi, Minister for School Education, Government of Tamil Nadu. Prof. Mohana Krishnan R , Director, Directorate of Sports, SRMIST, received the Certificate of Honour for Excellence in Sports Leadership & Governance Award conferred by the Physical Education Foundation of India (PEFI), recognised by the Ministry of Youth Affairs & Sports, Government of India. The award was presented by Shri Kamal Haasan, Member of Parliament, Rajya Sabha, and Thiru Anbil Mahesh Poyyamozhi , Minister for School Education, Government of Tamil Nadu, during the 2025 Award for Excellence in Sports. The award ceremony was held on 23rd December 2025 at Tiruchirappalli as part of the Award for Excellence in Sports 2025 celebrations. The award recognises Prof. Mohana Krishnan’s outstanding leadership and sustained contribution to the development of sports and physical education at SRM Institute of Science and Technology, where he has played a pivotal role in strengthening sports governance, athlete development, and institutional excellence in sports administration. His efforts have significantly enhanced SRMIST’s reputation as a leading institution for integrated academic and sports excellence. The ceremony was held in the presence of Dr. D. Prasanna Balaji, Secretary, PEFI – Tamil Nadu State Chapter, and Rtn. AKS Er Muruganandam M (MMM), President, PEFI – Tamil Nadu State Chapter. This national-level recognition reflects SRMIST’s strong commitment to excellence in sports leadership and governance through the institution’s well-structured Directorate of Sports, which has consistently promoted high standards in athlete development, infrastructure planning, and institutional sports administration. Under the leadership of Prof. Mohana Krishnan R, the Directorate of Sports at SRMIST has emerged as a model for integrating academic excellence with competitive sports, fostering a culture of discipline, inclusivity, and performance. His visionary approach and sustained efforts have played a prominent role in nurturing talent, strengthening structured sports systems, and positioning SRMIST as a significant contributor to the nation’s sporting ecosystem. Alumni Day 2025 at SRMIST: Where Journeys Reconnect and Legacies Continue Home Alumni Day 2025 at SRMIST: Where Journeys Reconnect and Legacies Continue SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) celebrated Alumni Day 2025 on 20th December 2025 at the Kattankulathur campus, bringing together over 1,200 alumni from across disciplines and campuses. The event reflected the institution’s strong and enduring relationship with its graduates and their continuing role in carrying SRMIST’s values into the wider world. Addressing the gathering, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar , Founder Chancellor, highlighted the collective spirit behind SRM’s growth. He noted that the institution did not grow because of one individual, but because every alumnus who passed through the campus carried its vision forward. His address emphasised the lasting impact of alumni contributions on SRMIST’s progress and reputation. The Vice Chancellor reinforced this shared responsibility, encouraging alumni to stay closely connected with their alma mater. Alumni were invited to mentor students, contribute to teaching and professional development initiatives, and actively support innovation and entrepreneurship through DEI initiatives, where over 50 start-ups are currently thriving. They were also urged to engage with the university’s career centre to guide and empower the next generation. Alumni Day 2025 featured reflective and inspiring accounts from former students who spoke about SRMIST’s role in shaping their lives. Mr. Selvaraj Srinivasan of the 1991 batch shared his journey from a rural classroom to global platforms, crediting SRM for laying the foundation for his aspirations. Ms. Vino Supraja, an alumna of the 2001 batch and the first architect from Vandavasi, reflected on how the campus nurtured her confidence and personal growth. A key highlight of the celebration was the Alumni Awards Ceremony, which recognised outstanding alumni under three categories: Distinguished Alumni, Eminent Alumni, and Young Achievers. The awardees were honoured for their excellence, leadership, and contributions across industry, academia, entrepreneurship, and social service. The celebration reaffirmed SRMIST’s commitment to lifelong alumni engagement, strengthening connections across generations and reinforcing the role of its global alumni community in shaping the institution’s enduring legacy. text: SRM School of Law Kattankulathur - Chennai Home SRM School of Law In the words of our Founder & Chancellor Law schools fulfill the noble obligation of imparting legal education to their students so that they can become competent, confident, and equipped to face effectively, all new challenges in their legal practice and research in this constantly evolving era, and concomitantly, to be committed to serving society with humanity, compassion, and humility. Law is one of the oldest, noblest, and greatest intellectual professions that demands holistic and focused education and guidance beyond the ‘four walls of the classroom’. One must know that behind each great jurist, judge, eminent lawyer, other exceptional professor, and legal professional is a strong law school and a dedicated fraternity of faculties committed to building these personalities. School of Law, SRM Institute of Science & Technology is one of the leading Law Schools in India recognized by BCI which was initiated with a commitment to delivering the highest standards in legal education and training. SRMIST has consistently ranked amongst the top-ranking multi-stream universities in India and abroad, with a vision to impart quality education that ensures the products of Law school stand par excellence. School of Law, SRMIST offers quality legal education in Undergraduate (UG), Postgraduate (PG), Doctor of Philosophy (PhD), and Diploma programs as well as Certificate Courses and Value Added Courses which are focused on developing legal knowledge in a wide variety of legal areas. SRM School of Law takes pride in developing and executing a strong curriculum and pedagogies that have been framed; keeping up contemporary standards and expectations. Placements and internships are strongly facilitated, due to which, the alumni of SRM School of Law have made us proud by occupying coveted positions in different fields. The strive towards excellence of SRM School of Law is also evident in the institution’s constant endeavor of building a team of committed and qualified teachers who are equipped to impart knowledge and training to the students such that they excel not only in academics but all spheres of life. SRM School of Law has a rich legacy, having completed a decade of its establishment, and making significant strides towards excellence. SRM School of Law was inaugurated in 2014 by the Honorable Chief Judge of the Supreme Court of Maryland, Mary Ellen Barbara along with the judicial delegates from the State of Maryland, USA which was led by the First Lady of State, Katie O’Malley. The School ties up with many foreign universities and institutions, which incorporates a rich exchange of knowledge and research activities. SRM Law School conducts two National-level Moot Court competitions annually in which students from different parts of the country take part. The industry-academic interface is a major part of teaching pedagogy. Today’s lawyer is supposed to have exposure to different disciplines like science & technology, from medicine to literature, and from arts to sports, SRMIST is a leader in all! Being a multidisciplinary university, law students enjoy the exclusive scope of exposure to other streams of learning added to the law. The multicultural atmosphere of the campus provides a great opportunity for the students to know different cultures as students from different parts of India and the world are studying here. The environment-friendly green campus adds additional energy to the atmosphere here at SRM. Hostel facilities with high-quality hygienic food, personal care, and safety make the students feel that they are not away from their homes. Students’ talents in sports and cultural activities are well recognized through special scholarships and training that propel students to excel in their respective areas of interest. As an institution striving to constantly aim higher, SRM School of Law equips, exposes, empowers, and enlightens the students to take up the challenges with confidence and commitment as they power forward in life. text: Mission Vision To emerge as a world-class Law School in disseminating knowledge and providing students with a unique learning experience in law, ethics, governance and policy-making that will best serve the world and betterment of mankind. Mission Acquire knowledge and learn through international alliances and collaborative initiatives to achieve global excellence in imparting legal education. Accomplish through rigorous academic and research environment to attain advance knowledge with a thrust on multidisciplinarity. Attract and invigorate scholars in a rewarding and inspiring environment by fostering freedom, empowerment, creativity, and innovation. Teaching & Learning Excellence SRM School of Law goes beyond textbooks. Our curriculum emphasizes real-world scenarios with practical application through case studies, simulations, and client-counseling exercises. This prepares students to be practice-ready from day one. To hone legal minds, the School actively participates in and hosts national-level moot court competitions. These competitions simulate courtrooms, giving students invaluable experience in legal research, persuasive writing, and oral arguments. Recognizing the globalized legal field, SRMIST fosters collaborations with foreign universities. This exposure to diverse legal systems broadens student horizons and prepares them for an interconnected world. The School prioritizes holistic development. They offer workshops beyond academics, fostering communication skills, legal ethics, and even leadership training. Additionally, the multicultural campus exposes students to diverse viewpoints, a valuable asset in today’s legal landscape. Understanding the importance of ethical lawyers, the college incorporates character-building initiatives. Workshops on legal ethics, social responsibility, and pro bono legal work instill a strong sense of ethical responsibility in students. 0 UG & PG programs 0 UG & PG Seats 0 MoUs with Abroad Law Schools Annual Report Newsletter career and Placement The Career Center in SRM Institute of Science and Technology plays a vital role in making SRM a favored destination for many national and international organizations to recruit fresh talent nurtured at the Institute. The excellent infrastructure of the Career Center facilitates activities like preplacement talks, written tests, group discussions, and interviews as per the requirements of the recruiting organizations. text: School of Public Health Empowering Global Health Leaders Shaping future health leaders through innovation, research, and global impact. MHS UG - Apply Now MHS PG - Apply Now Programs Placements Alumni Pedagogy Collaborations Projects Wellness Program Newsletter Advancing Global Health Through Innovation and Education School of Public Health The School of Public Health, established in 2007, is a premier institution dedicated to advancing public health education, training, and research. The school nurtures competent and inspired public health professionals, health managers, health data scientists, biostatisticians and thought leaders through cutting edge education technology and experiential learning methods. The students here get the opportunity to participate in ongoing research projects of the school and get hands on training and field exposure. The school fosters industry interface and international collaborations. At the School of Public Health, the faculty bring a wealth of field experience and relevant research into classroom, ensuring rigorous competency-based education. Through interdisciplinary coursework, hands-on learning opportunities, and collaborations with leading health organizations, we will prepare students to develop communication skills, project management skills, research and data analytics to grow professionally and contribute to improving health outcomes of populations. Our Master of Public Health (MPH) and BSc-MPH integrated programs are designed for those who are interested in designing and managing health projects. The MSc–Biostatistics and Epidemiology program is for those with a keen interest in research and data analytics. The MSc–Health Data Science program is for those interested in use of artificial intelligence, machine learning, algorithms, and cloud computing and big data analytics in healthcare domain. text: develop communication skills, project management skills, research and data analytics to grow professionally and contribute to improving health outcomes of populations. Our Master of Public Health (MPH) and BSc-MPH integrated programs are designed for those who are interested in designing and managing health projects. The MSc–Biostatistics and Epidemiology program is for those with a keen interest in research and data analytics. The MSc–Health Data Science program is for those interested in use of artificial intelligence, machine learning, algorithms, and cloud computing and big data analytics in healthcare domain. To be one of the most sought-after public health institutions in the country. Nurture competent and inspired public health professionals who will make meaningful contributions to improving the health of communities. Admission Procedure Applications for Master programs are invited in January each year through a notification on the Institute’s website. Interested candidates need to register on the SRM website and appear for a written examination held in April and June. Qualified candidates proceed to an interview. Upon selection, candidates are required to submit the certificates and specified fee by a given date. Classes begin by mid-July. Experience Life Beyond the Classroom, Discover, Connect, and Thrive in a Campus that Never Sleeps Campus Life at SRMIST The School of Public Health is located near Chennai at the Kattankulathur campus of the SRM Institute of Science & Technology. The campus offers five hostels – three for girls and two for boys, where accommodation is available on a first-come, first-served basis. Due to limited availability, interested students are advised to book their accommodation immediately after enrollment. Additionally, many paying-guest accommodations are available in surrounding areas. Smart Classrooms Arts & Clubs Sports & Fitness Diversity Vibrant College Life Anti-Ragging Committee Empowering Graduates with Outstanding Career Outcomes Placement MPH graduates generally begin their professional journey with mid-level positions such as program officer or research officer in a national or international NGO, CSR initiatives, research organization, or government health department. Progressing in their careers, they ascend to state-level manager, eventually reaching national-level management positions. Some specialize in specific areas like Monitoring, Learning and Evaluation, Research, Community Mobilization, Advocacy, TB, HIV/AIDS, Family Planning, Gender Equity, Environmental Health, Health Communication, Health Economics etc. Some students choose to pursue a Ph.D. and get into academia. text: Arts & Culture Explore More​ Contact Us Home Life at SRM Arts & Culture Arts & Culture It’s our firm belief at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University), that to enhance the skills of our students, they must be provided with opportunities to explore their creativity. Cultural programs are organized several times during the year to encourage creative pursuits and nurture talent. Students form teams and share responsibility in organizing the events. Milan A four-day national-level inter-college cultural festival, characterized by a footfall of 50,000+ students, encompasses a diverse array of over 100 competitions and high-caliber pro shows, including DJ nights, live music performances, and comedy nights featuring top-tier artists. This event draws participants from various states and districts, fostering a rich exchange of talent and cultural diversity. 2020 2019 2018 Shuru Shuru is a talent hunt festival organized by the Directorate of Student Affairs, featuring a range of competitions in various cultural art forms, including dance, music, creative arts, dramatics, fashion etc, specifically designed for first-year students from all departments. Additionally, the event includes host performances by clubs to enhance the entertainment experience for newcomers. 2020 2019 Jhalak Jhalak is an intracollege cultural event where students from diverse academic departments within the university showcase their talents. This event features participation from eight distinct houses, namely Agni, Astra, Brahmos, Nag, Prithvi, Aakash, Shaurya, and Trishul, who compete in various cultural activities. 2023 2018 Rendezvous Rendezvous is a club showcase event, representing approximately 16 diverse clubs encompassing areas such as dance, music, fashion, and dramatics. This event is dedicated to welcoming and engaging new students and is conducted by the Directorate of Student Affairs. 2023 Ace ACE is a talent hunt festival organized by the Directorate of Student Affairs, featuring a range of Sports events like Cricket, Football, Badminton, Table Tennis, etc, specifically designed for first-year students from all departments. Josh The Directorate of Student Affairs arranged JOSH’23, a cultural event for both teaching and non-teaching staff, with a participation of 450+ individuals. Butterflies Butterflies, an altruistic undertaking by the Directorate of Student Affairs seeks to instill inspiration and motivation in underprivileged children regarding education. This initiative offers a platform for students from government schools to exhibit their skills in sports, culture, literature, and more. It’s a day brimming with enjoyable activities, competitions, and rewards, with approximately 500 students taking part every year . Khel Khel

Leadership Team Home About Leadership Team Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar Founder Chancellor Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar founded SRM Group in 1968 and is respected as a Visionary for his service to the cause of Education and Public at large. He served as a Distinguished Parliamentarian representing Perambalur Lok Sabha constituency, Tamil Nadu. SRM is one of India’s most multicultural large institutions offering a truly multidisciplinary learning experience to students and researchers. It is his foresight and intent to empower through education that he nurtured SRM Institute of Science and Technology into an Institute of Higher Learning with high set of standards for professional education from as early as 1985 (since its inception). He visualizes God in the smiles of the disenfranchised and neglected and is seeing to it that they are empowered with full scholarships. This is instrumental in developing their life standards and dignity. He is notably supportive of enabling empowerment through education to the meritorious students in Tamil Nadu. SRM Central Instrumentation Facility (SCIF) Home Research SCIF Overview SRMIST is continually investing and setting up laboratories in addition to enhancing the lab equipments and softwares on a regular basis. As a measure of its commitment to creating meaningful and society uplifting impact, SRMIST is open to collaborating with anybody within SRMIST as well as externally among the industry and academic segment. You are welcome to access this SCIF portal that consolidates the state-of-the-art research facilities available under the Directorate of Research in SRMIST. You may directly put through your request to us to successfully reserve your access to certain facility / equipment at SRMIST. Dean (Research) The Head, SRM Central Instrumentation Facility Email: statcentre@srmist.edu.in. Welcome to SCIF The SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) for over 3 decades now invested in bringing best of the learning and research enablers to its students, scholars and faculty members. SRMIST’s legacy of research excellence and impact has made a mark in the society at large and especially among the industry and government. Faculty members are actively encouraged to undertake high value, world-class research projects from various government and private sector. SRMIST given its legacy and scale of operations is ever so open to contributing to research endeavors of anybody within SRMIST or externally. It gives a great platform for our faculty members and scholars to be of assistance to other fellow researchers from the industry or academia. We welcome you to benefit from the ecosystem SRMIST offers to you and send us your request for our support. We will be more than happy to collaborate with you in so far as offering our facilities and equipments for your research endeavors. Our SCIF wishes you the very best in your research and innovation efforts. Meet Our Staff Research Facilities at the Centre Center for Environmental Nuclear Research has the independent laboratory facilities with state-of-art equipment for its research work. Dining & Eateries Home SRM Hostels Dining & Eateries Mess Across every hostel, an exclusive mess facility is made available for breakfast, lunch and dinner under the safe and hygiene environment Mess Timinigs Working Days Breakfast 07.00 AM to 9.00 AM Lunch 11.30 AM to 1.30 PM Tea/ Snacks 04.30 PM to 5.30 PM Dinner 7.30 PM to 9.00 PM Holidays Breakfast 07.30 AM to 9.30 AM Lunch 12.00 Noon to 1.30 PM Tea/ Snacks 04.30 PM to 5.30 PM Dinner 7.30 PM to 9.00 PM Mess Rules Once a student joins the hostel, he / she is deemed to have become a member of the mess until he / she vacates the hostel officially No change of mess is permissible The mess is run by the management Strict discipline should be maintained in the dining hall Menu will be displayed on the notice board. Changes can be made based on the decision of mess committee. Day scholars will not be entertained as guests in the mess Inmates can entertain their parents as guests on prior information to the hostel authorities. The guest rate is Rs.80/- for breakfast, Rs. 100/- for Veg lunch, Rs. 40/- for Tea/Snacks & Rs. 100/- for dinner respectively. For special lunch/dinner the rate is Rs. 150/-. Food will not be served in rooms and the inmates are not supposed to take food to their rooms. If an inmate is ill, the deputy warden will make suitable arrangements. The inmates should not enter the kitchen Wasting food is a crime. No diner shall waste food. Paying mess bill does not entitle a diner to waste food. If any student is found wasting food he / she will be fined Rs 500/- for the first time. If the same student is found repeating the same mistake he / she will be expelled from the hostel. The system of self service will be followed in all the mess The quantity of food will be unlimited except in the case of special items All diners shall produce ID card and Mess card to dine every time in the mess Unauthorized people and non hostelers seen in the dining hall without receipts of payment will be charged a fine of Rs.2000/- Canteens SRMIST Canteen (Old Campus) SRMIST Food Joint, Fast Food & Super Market Location: Adjacent to the Main Entrance (Near gate No 4, Opp to the Potheri Railway Station)

SRM College of Pharmacy Home Medicine & Health Sciences SRM College of Pharmacy About Programs Placements Faculty Infrastructure Contact About College of Pharmacy SRM College of Pharmacy, SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University), Kattankulattur is one of the premier institutions for pharmaceutical education and research in the country. The college was started in the year 1992 affiliated to the Tamil Nadu Dr.M.G.R. Medical University and was later brought under the ambit of SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University) in 2005. The College offers B.Pharm, M.Pharm. (Pharmaceutical Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Analysis, Pharmaceutics, Pharmacology, Pharmacognosy, Pharmacy Practice, Pharmaceutical Quality Assurance and Pharmaceutical Regulatory affairs), PhD and Pharm.D and Pharm.D (Post baccalaureate) courses. The programs of the College are approved by the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) New Delhi. SRM Institute of Science and Technology (formerly known as SRM University) offers great opportunities in skilled pharmacists in pharmaceutical industries and health care MNC’s both in India and abroad. It is one of the leading institutions in India with an exceptional curriculum designed to prepare quality pharmacists with professional capabilities so as to reach great heights in the ratios healthcare system. The pharmacist of 21st century possess a significant role in managing drug therapy, counselling patients on proper use of medications and monitoring drug therapy outcomes for which the new Pharm.D program prepares the students. The faculty and staff are the pride of SRMCP, who by their expertise teach and promote the learning process and also serve as an inspiration to the students to their research activities. They mould the student community into the outstanding professional by the advanced teaching techniques and learning aids. The college is vested with state-of-the-art infrastructure and provides top-notch facilities to the budding pharmacists. The programme is supplemented by a refreshing breath of co-curricular and extra-curricular activities to ensure that the students acquire personality excellence and leadership qualities in addition to the technical expertise, which are vital to excel in a competitive world. (Pharmaceutical Chemistry, Pharmaceutical Analysis, Pharmaceutics, Pharmacology, Pharmacognosy, Pharmacy Practice, Pharmaceutical Quality Assurance and Pharmaceutical Regulatory affairs), PhD and Pharm.D and Pharm.D (Post baccalaureate) courses. Our students excel in Curricular, Extra-Curricular and Co-Curricular activities and showcase their talents. SRM College of Occupational Therapy Home Medicine & Health Sciences SRM College of Occupational Therapy About Programs Placements Faculty Infrastructure Contact About the College Occupational Therapy is a healthcare profession that enables people of all ages to be independent in their day-to-day activities. SRM College of Occupational Therapy was started in the academic year 1996 under the Tamil Nadu Dr. M.G.R. Medical University. In the year 2004, the college was brought under the ambit of SRM University, and the same year Postgraduate program was started. SRM College is the only occupational therapy college to offer BOT , MOT , and PhD programs in Tamil Nadu. It is one of the colleges in South India which is recognized by the All India Occupational Therapists’ Association (AIOTA), the National Governing body of the profession and World Federation of Occupational Therapists (WFOT), the apex professional body worldwide, and accredited by NAAC with graded A++. These recognitions give a distinct advantage of being accepted at par in the world of medicine, which allows students to seek higher studies or employment in abroad. Occupational Therapy in SRM is committed to guiding our students through a distinct learning process that allows the individual student to think, evaluate their own learning styles and experience learning through civic engagement in emerging practice areas and service-learning opportunities. We provide our students with exceptional opportunities to learn and grow as they transition from the classroom to successful careers in Occupational Therapy. 100% Job placement is guaranteed for students who graduate every year in BOT and MOT. 80% of our graduates are well placed in countries like USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Singapore, and Middle East. We have well-equipped clinical setup & research facilities. Tie up facilities for clinical training is being provided in major corporate hospitals such as SIMS Hospital, Vadapalani and other organizations such as NIEPMD at Muttukadu, Right Hospital, MDA – a special school for Dyslexia. Students of SRM College of Occupational Therapy have been placed in overseas for clinical placement at Latrobe University, Australia. Campus placement is done for USA & India through our SRM placement cell for major hospitals and companies. Community outreach program is carried out in villages in and around Kattankulathur where our faculty and students conduct Disability Survey and Health Education to provide Occupational Therapy intervention at doorstep. The faculty and staff of the College of Occupational Therapy mentor and inspire our students to think, care, create, and pursue justice in our world. We do this by preparing the next generation of leaders to practice innovative, evidence-based healthcare for our diverse global community. Our students are fully engaged throughout the learning process in their in-person and virtual classrooms, through interprofessional patient scenarios in our simulation facility, clinics and research labs, community outreach and advocacy. We are dedicated in preparing students for contemporary healthcare practice, including an interprofessional, team-based approach to person-centered care. I hope you will explore our website to discover your future path in healthcare. Programs Offered B.O.T Bachelor of Occupational Therapy trains students and helps them to become skilled professional occupational therapists, capable of undertaking independent/supervised practice. M.O.T The Master of Occupational Therapy provides students with skills and knowledge in treating clients who have difficulty participating in everyday life activities due to neurological, musculoskeletal or mental health issues. PhD The Ph.D. in Occupational Therapy is a research-focused program designed to advance clinical expertise and academic knowledge. It prepares scholars to contribute to rehabilitation and interdisciplinary healthcare. Placement Occupational Therapy profession provides a wide array of career options and avenues for specialization. We take great pride in the opportunities to secure placements in various countries such as the UK, Qatar, Dubai, Australia, New Zealand, and Canada. These achievements stand as a testament to the dedication and hard work of our graduates, showcasing their efforts in advancing the profession on a global scale. Collaborations Home Research Collaborations Collaborations Collaborations are the bedrock upon which our excellence in research is distinctly impactful and significant. We are able to work on emerging and frontier areas because we bring together the best and brightest from around the world to our par-take in research at SRMIST. NRC EQRC IIISM CENR Nanotech Research Center The Center has worked to establish technical & scientific collaborations with a number of national and international institutional and industrial collaborations. Collaborations National Academic collaboration with BiTS Pilani Goa Industry Collaboration with Sankara Nethralaya International academic collaborations with Toyo University, Japan, University of Nevada, Las Vegas, USA,Free University of Berlin, Berlin, Germany Earthquake Research Cell (EQRC) SRMIST established an Earthquake Research cell (EQRC) in July 2012 for Advanced Research on the causes for origin, occurrence and prediction of Earthquake Collaborations EQRC centre has international collaborations with Taiwan, Thailand, iraq, Australia and the BRICS Project Collaborations on EQRC Interdisciplinary Institute of Indian System of Medicine (IIISM) IIISM Established in 2008, this prestigious IIISM is at the forefront of promoting research in the field of traditional system of Indian Medicine, including Siddha, Ayurveda and Unani. Collaborations IIISM has signed a MoU with Banaras Hindu University, Varanasi and Adesh Institute of Medical Sciences, Punjab for conducting multi-centric clinical trials of the polyherbal formulations.The herbal drugs that successfully pass clinical evaluation will be manufactured by SRM Pharma Pvt. Ltd., Chennai. SRMIST has signed a MoU with M/S. SMO Connect Pvt. Ltd, Cochin, Kerala for conducting Phase-III Clinical trial on modern medicine in the area of Endocrinology, Pulmonology, Cardiology, Dermatology, Rheumatology etc Center for Environmental Nuclear Research (CENR) CENR as a Center was established in 2009 with grants from the Department of Atomic Energy, Board of Research in Nuclear Science (BRNS), Mumbai and with the extensive support from Directorate of Research, SRMIST Collaborations CENR maintains strong collaborative links with National research organizations such as BARC Mumbai, Indira Gandhi Centre For Atomic Research, Kalpakkam,Zoological Survey of India, Chennai,Kings Institute of Preventive Medicine, Chennai ,Industries such as Synkromax Biotech Pvt Ltd., Chennai, Hatsun Agro Products, Chennai and International research organizations such as National Institute of Health (NIH) / National Cancer Institute (NCI) – USA, National University of Singapore, Singapore, University of Basel , Switzerland and many more. SRMIST Organises World Record Event with 3,032 Participants to Celebrate 150 Years of Vande Mataram Home SRMIST Organises World Record Event with 3,032 Participants to Celebrate 150 Years of Vande Mataram SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) organised a world record attempt to commemorate the 150th anniversary of the national song “Vande Mataram.” The initiative was carried out in alignment with the vision of the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India and under the guidance of the Founder Chancellor, Dr. T.R. Paarivendhar. As part of the attempt, 3,032 participants, comprising students, faculty members, and non-teaching staff, came together to form the Largest Human Formation Depicting “VANDE MATARAM.” Participants were dressed in tricolour shades, symbolising unity and national identity. The world record attempt was held on 06 January 2026, in the presence of representatives from the World Record Union. Speaking on the occasion, Dr. S. Ponnusamy , Registrar, SRMIST said, “This world record attempt reflects the collective effort of the SRMIST community. By coming together for this formation, we have marked a moment that reflects discipline, participation, and shared responsibility in commemorating 150 years of Vande Mataram.” Dr. Karu Nagarasan, Head, Tamil Perayam, SRMIST, stated, “Vande Mataram is deeply rooted in India’s cultural and linguistic heritage. Through this attempt, we honour its 150-year legacy and express it through collective human participation.” The event was attended by Dr. R. Mohana Krishnan , Director, Directorate of Sports, SRMIST; Dr. V. Thirumurugan , Associate Director – Campus Administration, SRMIST; Dr. A. Duraisamy , Dean, Faculty of Science and Humanities, SRMIST; Dr. Shanthichitra K ., Chairperson (Student Outreach and Experience), Faculty of Science & Humanities, SRMIST; Dr. P. Jaiganesh , Secretary, Tamil Perayam, SRMIST. The initiative reflects SRMIST’s emphasis on collective engagement and national awareness while commemorating a significant milestone in the legacy of Vande Mataram. text: SRM College of Agricultural Sciences Baburayanpettai - Chengalpattu Accredited by ICAR, New Delhi Home College of Agriculture Sciences SRM College of Agricultural Sciences About Programs Placements Contact About SRMCAS SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) established the Faculty of Agricultural Sciences at Main campus, Kattankulathur by starting B.Sc. (Hons.) Horticulture degree program in 2018 and B.Sc. (Hons.) Agriculture degree program in 2019 which was approved by the Board of Mangement. Later, in 2021 the Faculty of Agricultural Sciences was shifted to Baburayanpettai, Chengalpattu District and started functioning as SRM College of Agricultural Sciences (SRMCAS) in an area of 400+ acres, which is 68 km from the Main campus SRMIST, Kattankulathur, by covering the urban, semi-urban, and rural areas. More on CAS College of Agricultural Sciences Board Of Studies Awards And Accolades ICAR - SSR Sample Analysis Service Student Clubs, Professional Chapters And Association Bodies Extension Students Corner Alumni Connect Sports IQAC IQAC Members Centralized Lab Green Campus 2018 Established 882 Students 88 Faculties 150 + Publications 8 Funded Projects 10 Patents Vision Emerge as an International Institution to provide science based agricultural education and related process to younger generation both in rural and urban sectors for Youth – led Agriculture in India to revolutionise Agriculture as a way of business. Mission Empower younger generation in quality agricultural education to learn cutting – edge farming technologies to address the needs of different stakeholders viz., farmers, employers, industries and society. Strengthen higher education in Agriculture/Horticulture on par with international standards for innovative research, teaching and extension Promote advanced technologies including Artificial Intelligence, Hi-tech Agriculture/Horticulture, digital agriculture, climate smart farming to ensure sustainability in production and productivity Effectively transfer technology from lab to land to ensure water and food security, augment farmers income and standard of living of the farming community Dr. M. Jawaharlal Dean Welcome message The overall mission of the College of Agricultural Sciences is to provide knowledge and support for teaching, conducting research and capacity-building on the principles and practices for climate-smart, sustainable agriculture to produce healthy food with higher factor productivity and livelihood security. It is with a sense of commitment that our Founder & Chancellor Dr. T R Paarivendhar has personally seen to it that we empower and benefit farm community and food sector at large through extension research, pedagogy that engages students and researchers with on-field experience and lab facilities par excellence to generate pathbreaking interventions.

The Holistic Development Of The Students Makes SRM Graduates Stand Apart. We Are Passionate About Grooming The Students Not Only As Efficient Professionals But Also As Responsible Citizens. Future focus Agroecology Agroecology is an integrated approach that simultaneously applies ecological and social concepts and principles to the design and management of food and agricultural systems. It seeks to optimize the interactions between plants, animals, humans and the environment while taking into consideration the social aspects that need to be addressed for a sustainable and fair food system. More recently, agroecology has entered the discourse of international and UN institutions. Realizing the importance of the subject SRMIST has included agroecology in the curriculum of the UG programmes in Agriculture and Horticulture. A new course “Introduction to Agroecology” has been introduced for both horticulture and agriculture programmes for the first time in India. Crop Health A new division of Crop Health is being introduced wherein, the following subjects are brought under: Plant Biochemistry, Crop Physiology, Agricultural Entomology, Plant Pathology, Soil Fertility and Weed Management which are related to the well-being and growth of the crops. Infrastructure & Facilities SRM Institute of Science and Technology has been consistent to maintain excellence in the academic standard since its inception. Learning is an Enjoyable experience here, with experienced Faculty, Inspiring Libraries and Stimulating discussion rooms, Large Playgrounds and State-of-the-art Labs. Laboratory SRMIST is always investing in creating labs and provide our students an experience of handling the various advanced and futuristic equipments, instruments, softwares and ingredients that are in-use in a real life scenario in the companies and research organizations. Hands on Learning Our teaching and learning processes at College of Agricultural and Horticulture Sciences at SRMIST is extremely hands on where students get to apply the inputs from classroom on-the-field. Exposure visits for the students are organized to expose the students to agricultural institutions. Urban Farm Center With the fast paced transition to digital, SRMIST is an early pioneer to give our students exposure to state of the art studio facilities. He advises on AI policy, focusing on ensuring its development aligns with ethical standards, public welfare, and security. Krishnan plays a key role in shaping government strategies related to AI governance, regulation, and its impact on society. Before joining the White House, he held leadership positions in the tech industry, collaborating with companies, regulators, and think tanks. His work addresses critical challenges like AI ethics, bias, data privacy, and the economic implications of AI. Krishnan bridges the gap between technology innovation and policy-making, guiding the U.S. AI strategy. B.Tech. Shri Ashok Kumar Malhotra, Mission Director of the National Technical Textiles Mission (NTTM), leads India’s initiative to advance technical textiles under the Ministry of Textiles. With expertise in Textile Technology, AI, and Management, he has spearheaded over 168 research projects worth ₹510 crore, fostering industry-academia collaboration. His work emphasizes applications in healthcare, geotextiles, automotive, defence, aerospace, and sports. Under his leadership, NTTM has established sector-specific Centres of Excellence, including SITRA for medical textiles and ATIRA for composites. Shri Malhotra also promotes exports through partnerships with MATEXIL and aligns Indian products with global standards. His efforts focus on building a complete ecosystem, integrating manufacturing, research, and skill development. By addressing challenges such as low domestic penetration of technical textiles, he aims to position India as a global leader. Shri Malhotra envisions India becoming the third-largest technical textiles market by 2047, driven by innovation-led growth and strategic international collaborations. B.E (CSE) Year: 1999 Business Leader Mr. Ashok Ramachandran Group CEO, REE Corporation Ashok Ramachandran is a seasoned global business leader and Group CEO of REE Corporation, Vietnam, leading a diversified billion-dollar conglomerate across Energy, Water, EPC, Air Conditioning, and Real Estate. An ET 40 Under Forty awardee, he is known for driving strategy, transformation, and end-to-end P&L leadership across mature and emerging markets. With over two decades of international experience, he has held senior leadership roles, including COO & Board Member at JSW Energy, President & CEO of Schindler India, and Managing Director for Schindler in Vietnam, Malaysia, and Brunei, as well as regional leadership roles in Australia. An engineering professional by training, Ashok specializes in change management, negotiation, talent development, and operational excellence, and is an active LinkedIn thought leader sharing personal views on leadership and success. Complaints & Redressal Home Life at SRM Complaints & Redressal Student Grievance Redressal Committee (SGRC) SRMIST has constituted a Committee for handling and solving the grievance to express constructive & genuine concerns/grievances. To promote and maintain a sustainable, unbiased & Positive Educational Environment. It involves a process of investigation in which ‘Student’s Grievance Redressal Committee’ enquires and analyses the nature and pattern of the grievances in a strictly confidential manner. UGC Regulations, 2023 Definition Grievance Redressal Cell SRMIST has constituted a Cell for handling and solving the grievance to express constructive & genuine concerns/grievances. Grievance Portal Internal Committee The committee’s primary responsibilities include handling complaints, conducting inquiries, and organizing gender sensitization programs. It operates in accordance with the UGC (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal of Sexual Harassment of Women Employees and Students in Higher Educational Institutions) Regulation, 2015. The ICC plays a crucial role in fostering a safe and inclusive environment for employees and students, ensuring that incidents of gender-based harassment are effectively addressed and prevented. Internal Committee Women's Grievance Redressal Committee SRMIST established a Women’s Grievance Cell to address any grievances of the female students, staff, and faculty. We are committed to provide a fully confidential and supportive environment to all the female members of our campus community. Women's Grievance Redressal Committee

Directorate of Communications The Directorate of Communications at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) is a department responsible for managing and facilitating communication efforts within the institution. This includes overseeing various aspects of communication such as public relations, media relations, internal communication, and branding. Home Directorate of Communications Welcome to Directorate of Communications The Directorate of Communications at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) is a department responsible for managing and facilitating communication efforts within the institution. This includes overseeing various aspects of communication such as public relations, media relations, internal communication, and branding. The primary role of the Directorate of Communications is to ensure effective and consistent communication between the university and the students, faculty, staff, alumni, media outlets, and the general public. This department works to promote the university’s achievements, initiatives, and events through various communication channels such as press releases, social media, official websites, and more.The Directorate of Communications plays a crucial role in maintaining a positive and transparent image of SRMIST with its diverse audience. Additionally, the Directorate of Communications collaborates with different departments and teams within the university to create and implement communication strategies that align with the institution’s goals and values. Mission The mission of the Directorate of Communications at SRMIST is to strategically manage and enhance communication across all platforms. We are dedicated to portraying SRMIST’s achievements, innovations, and contributions accurately and effectively to the world. Through transparent and engaging communication, we aim to build a strong sense of community and pride within the SRMIST family while upholding the institution’s values of excellence, integrity, and inclusivity. Vision Our vision is to establish SRMIST as a recognized global leader not only in education and research but also in effective communication. We envision a future where our communication efforts transcend boundaries, resonating with audiences worldwide. By showcasing the diverse talents, accomplishments, and advancements within our institution, we aspire to inspire, inform, and engage people from all walks of life. Through innovative communication strategies, we strive to amplify SRMIST’s positive impact on society, driving progress and collaboration on a global scale. Mr. Dev Konwar Director Directorate of Communications director.communications@srmist.edu.in Director's Message The core role of communication in the realm of higher education is to be the facilitator to thought leadership and institution building. Mr. Dev Konwar Director Directorate of Communications director.communications@srmist.edu.in Director's Message The core role of communication in the realm of higher education is to be the facilitator to thought leadership and institution building. The dynamics of communications and consumer behaviour has changed overnight. In a VUCA world where the audiences engagement has seen a sharp increase in digital media and, social; it is all the more significant for higher education entities to be relevant and be transparent in all platforms of engagement. We at SRMIST, it is our endeavour to communicate the essence of this great institution building through: Excellence, Integrity and Academic Freedom Global Vision and Local Commitment Inclusiveness, Diversity, Compassion and Mutual Respect ​In our relentless pursuit of excellence and institution building; communication is a powerful tool where all ethos needs to be reflected; be it our thought leadership, or our student journey, our accolades and, achievements. We the SRM! Let’s tell a tale of belief, glory and, transformation. “ The most important thing in communication is hearing what isn’t said. ” – Peter Drucker

Directorate of Sports At SRMIST, sports are a vital part of holistic education, promoting fitness, teamwork, and excellence. Guided by Founder Chancellor Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar’s vision, SRMIST provides world-class sports facilities, professional coaching, and opportunities for athletes to shine at national and international levels. Home Sports At SRMIST, sports are an essential part of holistic education and student life. Guided by the vision of our Founder Chancellor, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar , SRMIST aims to nurture excellence both in academics and athletics. The University offers world-class sports facilities including spacious playgrounds, indoor stadiums, fitness centers, and the state-of-the-art Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar Aquatic Complex. With professional coaching, modern infrastructure, and structured training programs, SRMIST encourages students to excel in national and international tournaments. Annual intercollegiate events and intramural competitions foster teamwork, leadership, and sportsmanship across all disciplines. Through Sports Quota admissions and scholarships, talented athletes are recognized and supported to achieve their dreams. At SRMIST, sports are not just recreational activities—they are a way of life that builds discipline, determination, and character, truly reflecting the Founder Chancellor’s vision of empowering youth through education and sport. Excel at Tamil Nadu State Weightlifting Championship Sports Achievements SRMIST’s Reshika Wins Gold at All India Junior National Badminton Championship Sports Achievements SRMIST Swimmer Dominates 36th South Zone Aquatic Championship with 6 Golds and 1 Silver Sports Achievements SRMIST Fencers Secure Bronze at Senior National Championship Sports Achievements Silver Medal Triumph at National Table Tennis Tournament Sports Achievements SRMIST Judo Men’s Team Emerges Overall Champion at South West Zone Inter-University Championship 2025 Sports Achievements Bronze Medal Glory at 11th GT Open Indoor World Archery Series International Standard Swimming Pool SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) boasts an international standard swimming pool at its Kattankulathur campus. This Olympic-sized facility, measuring 50 meters in length, is equipped with multiple lanes and advanced filtration systems to ensure a top-notch swimming experience. It serves as a hub for competitive training, recreational activities, and campus events, promoting a culture of fitness and well-being among students. With safety personnel on-site, the pool offers a secure environment for both professional athletes and casual swimmers. Dr. T.R.P Aquatic Complex Outdoor Games Most popular sports like basketball, vollyball, football, cricket, tennis, kabbadi, badminton are played by a large number of students in the evenings as well as early morning in our beautiful sprawling outdoor grounds and courts. Indoor Stadiums Indoor sports activity is vibrant and our students have access to a vast range of indoor sports like Table Tennis, Shuttle, and other fitness activitis. Fitness Center For students to stay fit and healthy, all hostels are equipped with gym and fitness centre facilities – treadmill, exercise bikes and more of the latest fitness equipment. The central gym for men at SRM is also equipped with body building equipment. All gyms and fitness centers are open both in the morning and evening hours. A healthy body and a healthy mind with Yoga 100s of students and staff of SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) participated in the virtual celebration of International Day of Yoga. Large scale Yoga programs are held in the many facilities within the large sprawling campus of SRMIST including in its multipurpose indoor stadium, lawns, playgrounds, auditoriums. Renowned guests and Yoga specialists often visit our campus every year to help our students and staff master the ancient Indian gift to the world’s wellbeing and health. mind with Yoga 100s of students and staff of SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) participated in the virtual celebration of International Day of Yoga. Large scale Yoga programs are held in the many facilities within the large sprawling campus of SRMIST including in its multipurpose indoor stadium, lawns, playgrounds, auditoriums. Renowned guests and Yoga specialists often visit our campus every year to help our students and staff master the ancient Indian gift to the world’s wellbeing and health. There is also students run Yoga club within SRMIST Sporting Achievements SRM Institute of Science and Technology values the inspiration and accomplishments that sports enable to our students and our institution. It is with this unflinching conviction that we are dedicated to keep scaling up our facilities, support, funding and commitment towards excellence in sports. We don’t believe in living in the past laurels alone. We take them as proof of our potential for the future. Our constant and consistent victories of our students in various championships across the nation and internationally keep us forge ahead in this direction to foster excellence. It is our dedicated champion coaches, support staff with the SRMIST’s Leadership who bring out the best out of our hard working and committed sportspersons/students. To get admission under the Sports Quota, SRMIST holds trials at its Kattankulathur campus. Those who are seeking to join Engineering, Management, Science and Humanities, B.Sc., Physical Education, Diploma Yoga & MSc Yoga, Law, Hotel Management and Health Sciences, through Sports Quota can take part in these trials after registering themselves in the prescribed online registration form that is announced during admissions season each year. SRMIST Startup Ecosystem SRMIST Startup Ecosystem a dynamic and nurturing environment designed to transform innovative ideas into successful ventures. At SRMIST, we believe in fostering entrepreneurial spirit and providing our students, faculty, and alumni with the resources and support they need to turn their visions into reality. Home SRMIST Startup Ecosystem About SRMIST Startup Ecosystem The SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) Startup Ecosystem fosters innovation and entrepreneurship by providing a comprehensive support system for budding entrepreneurs. Our state-of-the-art incubation centers offer essential resources, while our extensive mentorship network connects startups with industry experts. We facilitate access to funding through venture capital, angel investors, and grants. Regular workshops and training sessions equip entrepreneurs with vital skills. Startups benefit from collaboration with our esteemed faculty and researchers, driving cutting-edge solutions. SRMIST has a proud history of nurturing successful startups recognized nationally and internationally. Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) SRMIST is committed to fostering global partnerships that enhance educational, research, and cultural exchange. Our Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs) with leading universities, research institutions, and industry partners worldwide reflect this commitment. These strategic alliances enable us to offer our students and faculty unparalleled opportunities for growth, innovation, and collaboration. Home SRMIST Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) About The Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) at SRM Institute of Science and Technology (SRMIST) plays a pivotal role in shaping our global and national collaborations. These strategic partnerships with leading academic institutions, research centers, and industry giants enable SRMIST to elevate its educational and research endeavors. Through a network of MoUs that stretches across the globe, SRMIST ensures that our students and faculty are integrated into an international academic community. These partnerships provide invaluable opportunities for global exposure, cross-cultural experiences, and access to cutting-edge resources and expertise, enriching the university’s academic and research landscape. Previous Next Strategic Collaborations SRMIST’s MoUs are designed to foster mutually beneficial relationships that contribute to academic growth, research advancements, and societal impact. These collaborations enable us to: Expand Academic Opportunities Joint degree programs, student and faculty exchanges, and specialized training courses to provide a global academic perspective. Enhance Research Capabilities Collaborative research projects that push the boundaries of knowledge, leading to groundbreaking discoveries. Facilitate Industry Engagement Partnerships with industry leaders to offer hands-on experience, internships, and real-world problem-solving opportunities. Promote Innovation Encouraging innovation through joint ventures in technology development, intellectual property, and entrepreneurship initiatives. This should be done within 14 days of arrival in India, irrespective of their actual period of stay. Foreigners visiting India on any other category of long-term VISA valid for more than 180 days are not required to register themselves if their actual stay does not exceed 180 days on each visit. If such a foreigner intends to stay in India for more than 180 days during a particular visit, he/ she should get registered within 180 days of arrival in India. Pakistan nationals are required to register within 24 hours of their arrival in India.

Women’s Grievance Redressal Committee Home Life at SRM Complaints & Redressal Women’s Grievance Redressal Committee SRMIST established a Women’s Grievance Cell to address any grievances of the female students, staff, and faculty. We are committed to provide a fully confidential and supportive environment to all the female members of our campus community. The mandate of this Cell at the institute is to address: ✓ The facilities and needs of the women faculty, staff and students ✓ Problems arising out of any gender-related discrimination To this end, SRMIST organizes awareness camps, meetings, and seminars while promoting the work of this cell to allow recognition of the rights of women. This right to equality has been provided by the Constitution of India that includes the right to equality before the law, the prohibition of any kind of discrimination related to gender as well as providing equal opportunities in the academic field and their careers. Further Details To be updated shortly To get updates/advisories at regular intervals for prevention of cyber crimes, please follow Ministry of Home Affairs’ National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal MHA's National Cyber Crime Reporting Potal Twitter Handle @CyberDost as advised by Ministry of Home Affairs & AICTE Twitter @CyberDost

text: Faculty of Management Kattankulathur - Chennai We Are One of The Largest, Most Diverse Leading B-Schools in India Know More The Faculty of Management (FoM), a leading Business School in India, offers industry-relevant programmes designed to address business and societal challenges through academic excellence, research, and innovation. SRM FoM integrates live data, business simulations, case studies, and research into its curriculum, supported by top-tier infrastructure such as the FINTECH Lab, Ideation Cell, AI and Business Analytics Centre, and Social Entrepreneurship Cell. The FINTECH Lab, equipped with advanced tools like Thomson Reuters Eikon, CMIE Prowess IQ, and CRISIL Research Database, ensures an engaging and immersive learning experience. Home Career Centre Gateway To Great Careers Career Centre is the centre point of career development, interpersonal skills and placement activities. Career Centre is a target recruiting ground for all major recruiters and has strong linkages with industry and is highly regarded by employers. The Career Centre in SRM Institute of Science and Technology plays a vital role in making SRM a favoured destination for many national and international organizations to recruit fresh talent nurtured at the Institute. Continuous institute-industry interaction, Pre-Joining learning initiatives, participating in industrial exhibitions, fairs, seminars to promote useful interaction with the industries and alumni activities are some of the initiatives of Placement Office. The excellent infrastructure of the Career Centre facilitates activities like pre-placement talks, written tests, group discussions, and interviews as per the requirements of the recruiting organizations. *2025 Batch (As on 03rd January 2026) 1 + Number of Roles + Offers + High Paying Offers LPA Highest CTC 0 LPA Average CTC Dr. N.Venkata Sastry Director, Career Centre T: +91-44 -27455774 (D) / 27452270 Extn: 7301 Director's Message The Career Centre at SRMIST encompasses the Placement Office and the Career Development Centre. Career Development Centre helps to identify the interests and capabilities of students in relation to their career goals and academic progression. Students are encouraged to pursue their passion. Intensive coaching and training is provided in personality development and in shaping their leadership qualities. The Placement Office besides, placement and training holds workshops, events like Hackathons, Quiz etc., conferences, talks by expert, internship, industrial visits to enable students to be up-to-date with contemporary trends and be ‘Market Ready’ by the time of graduation. We are ably supported and guided by our Founder & Chancellor, Dr. T. R. Paarivendhar, who in his inimitable spirit of service has set for us a benchmark of tradition of excellence. We are therefore able to initiate some of the firsts in the Indian higher education system. For instance, our CDC collaborates with Career Center to focus on raising employability of our students. I look forward to personally seeing you in our campus and satiate your leadership talent needs. I got placed in a dream company Optum with a handsome package. This has been made possible for me by the placement cell's continued support throughout the placement procedure.
